title: Province of Saskatchewan - Canada.ca
content:
## Province of Saskatchewan
The Canada Revenue Agency administers the Saskatchewan low‑income tax credit (SLITC) for the Province of Saskatchewan. The SLITC is a program related to the Canada Groceries and Essentials Benefit (CGEB) (formerly known as the goods and services tax/harmonized sales tax (GST/HST) credit).

## Saskatchewan low-income tax credit (SLITC)
This credit is a tax-free amount paid to help Saskatchewan residents with low and modest incomes.
For July 2026 to June 2027, this program provides $460 for an individual, $460 for a spouse or common–law partner (or for an eligible dependant), and $181 per child (maximum of two children), or an annual credit of up to $1,282 per family. The credit starts to be reduced when the adjusted family net income is more than $39,345. Families with adjusted family net income between $39,345 and $81,668 may get part of the credit.
The payments are combined with the quarterly payments of the federal CGEB.
The Saskatchewan low–income tax credit program is fully funded by the Saskatchewan provincial government.
- RC4210 – Canada Groceries and Essentials Benefit pamphlet
